NSE:GREAVESCOT.name: Greaves Cotton Ltd
NSE:GREAVESCOT.businessSummary: Greaves Cotton is a diversified engineering company making engines, gensets, and EV components for three-wheelers. It has pivoted toward electric mobility through its Ampere EV brand.

notes.announcementTitle: Incorporation of WOS in Dubai UAE
notes.insight: Greaves Cotton has incorporated a wholly owned subsidiary Greaves International Trading FZE in Dubai UAE (effective 18 Jun 2026). This signals intent to build an international distribution / export channel, likely for EV components or power products. Worth monitoring for revenue contribution in 6–12 months.

notes.announcementTitle: Incorporation of WOS in Dubai UAE (duplicate filing)
notes.insight: Duplicate filing for the same Greaves International Trading FZE (Dubai) WOS incorporation announced today. Same event as bv1swc09abyci8pv46bbc140.pdf — no new information. Greaves Cotton is building international trading infrastructure in UAE, consistent with their EV and power products export ambitions.

notes.announcementTitle: Communication on TDS Deduction on Dividend to Shareholders
notes.insight: Greaves Cotton has communicated to shareholders about TDS deductions applicable on dividend payments. This is a compliance-driven communication confirming a dividend payout is imminent or has been processed. The dividend itself is a positive income return for shareholders — confirms the company is profitable enough to return cash.

notes.announcementTitle: Ampere Crosses 4 Lakh Cumulative Units; FY26 Growth 51% YoY, Market Share 4.4%
notes.insight: Greaves Electric Mobility (GEML)'s Ampere brand crossed the 4 lakh cumulative scooter milestone. Key operating data: FY26 sales grew 51% YoY; market share expanded from 3.6% in FY25 to 4.4% in FY26 in India's electric 2-wheeler market; dealer network grew 12% in FY26. Product line operates in the sub-₹1 lakh segment on 100% LFP battery platform. This positions Ampere as a sub-Ola/Ather mass-market player targeting price-sensitive buyers. The 51% growth rate is strong but base was small — at 4.4% market share, GEML is still a distant #4/#5 player behind Ola, TVS iQube, and Bajaj Chetak. The key question is whether this momentum is margin-positive or subsidy-driven. Monitor GEML's EBITDA contribution to the parent in upcoming Greaves Cotton quarterly results.

NSE:INOXINDIA.name: INOX India Ltd
NSE:INOXINDIA.businessSummary: INOX India is a leading manufacturer of cryogenic equipment — tanks, vessels, and systems for industrial gases, LNG, and space/defence applications. It serves global markets including NASA and ISRO.

notes.announcementTitle: Outcome of AGM — Resolutions Passed
notes.insight: INOX India has disclosed the outcome of its Annual General Meeting. Key things to watch in the AGM outcome: re-appointment of directors (especially independent directors), dividend ratification, and any special resolutions (related to borrowing limits, buybacks, or fundraise authorizations). AGM outcomes are procedurally routine unless a special resolution carries strategic significance for capital allocation or governance.

NSE:BLISSGVS.name: Bliss GVS Pharma Ltd
NSE:BLISSGVS.businessSummary: Bliss GVS Pharma is a specialty pharmaceutical manufacturer focused on suppositories, soft gelatin capsules, and anti-malarial drugs. It has a significant Africa/emerging markets export business.

notes.announcementTitle: Record Date Fixed for Final Dividend FY2025-26
notes.insight: Bliss GVS Pharma has fixed a record date for payment of final dividend for FY ended March 31, 2026, with the 41st AGM scheduled July 15, 2026. The dividend quantum was not disclosed in this filing. This signals the company is distributing profits for FY26 and the AGM will ratify the dividend. For shareholders, ensure shareholding is recorded before the record date to be eligible. Not a material fundamental development — monitor the AGM outcome and FY26 annual results for more substance.

notes.announcementTitle: Credit Rating Affirmed: IND AA+/Stable by India Ratings
notes.insight: India Ratings has affirmed Sona BLW's bank loan facilities at IND AA+/Stable/A1+ (bank facilities reduced from ₹9,250M to ₹7,250M, indicating lower debt usage). Key data from the rating report: FY26 consolidated revenue ₹44,495M (+26% YoY), EBITDA ₹10,812M (margin 24.3% vs 27.3% in FY25 — margin dip from railway division integration which has lower margins). Order book ₹2,37,000M as of March 31, 2026 (70% EV, 10-year execution horizon). Interest coverage 46x. Revenue growth guidance for FY27 at 20-25% YoY and FY28 at 15-20% YoY. The rating and underlying metrics confirm Sona BLW is firing strongly — EV order book ramp-up, railway diversification post-Escorts Kubota acquisition, and a near-pristine balance sheet. Margin compression (24.3% vs 28%+ earlier) is a watch point as railway integration continues. Monitor EBITDA margin trajectory over FY27 as railway revenue scales.

NSE:SHRIPISTON.name: Shriram Pistons & Rings Ltd
NSE:SHRIPISTON.businessSummary: SPR Auto Technologies (formerly Shriram Pistons & Rings) manufactures pistons, rings, and engine components for automotive OEMs. A key Tier-1 auto-ancillary supplier in India.

notes.announcementTitle: Communication to Shareholders Regarding TDS/Withholding Tax on Dividend
notes.insight: The board has recommended a final dividend of Rs. 5/- per share (face value Rs. 10/-) for FY2025-26, subject to shareholder approval at the 62nd AGM on July 27, 2026; record date is July 20, 2026. This routine TDS communication confirms a dividend will be paid by August 25, 2026 — positive for income investors. It also notes the company has been renamed SPR Auto Technologies Limited, which may signal a broader brand repositioning. Watch for the AGM outcome and any management commentary on the name change rationale.

notes.announcementTitle: Investor meet outcome: Mahindra MF and Sundaram MF (virtual 1:1, Jun 24)
notes.insight: SPR Auto Technologies (formerly Shriram Pistons & Rings) management completed virtual one-on-one meetings with Mahindra Mutual Fund and Sundaram Mutual Fund on June 24, 2026 — a follow-on to the schedule intimated June 23. Both are domestic fund houses. No UPSI shared; standard investor presentation material used. Two DII fund houses engaging via 1:1 meetings is a mild accumulation signal — suggests active diligence rather than passive index-tracking. Watch for changes in Mahindra MF / Sundaram MF holdings in the next shareholding disclosure (Q1 FY27 SHP).

NSE:SAREGAMA.name: Saregama India Ltd
NSE:SAREGAMA.businessSummary: Saregama India is the custodian of one of the world's largest music archives (Hindi, regional), and also produces content via Yoodlee Films. Its Carvaan retro music player is a well-known consumer product.

notes.announcementTitle: Disclosure under SEBI Takeover Regulations — Composure Services Pvt Ltd
notes.insight: Composure Services Pvt Ltd filed a Regulation 31(4) disclosure under the SEBI Takeover Regulations, indicating that an entity has crossed or is close to crossing a significant shareholding threshold (typically 5%, 10%, or a control threshold). This type of SAST disclosure is a signal that a strategic or financial investor is building or adjusting a stake in Saregama. If it marks a meaningful stake increase, it could indicate growing investor interest in Saregama's content IP and digital licensing business. The counterparty identity (Composure Services) is not widely known — monitor for further stake disclosures or open market purchases in subsequent filings.

notes.announcementTitle: SEBI Takeover Regulations Disclosures — Stake Change (2 filings)
notes.insight: Saregama has filed two SEBI SAST Reg 29 disclosures indicating promoter or PAC shareholding changes. The RPG Group (promoters) holding pattern changes are worth tracking — any incremental buying signals promoter conviction in the music streaming monetization story, while selling could flag profit-booking at elevated valuations. Watch for cumulative quantum.

notes.announcementTitle: New CFO Abhishek Kapoor (from Sula Vineyards) joins Jul 15; interim CFO from Jun 24
notes.insight: Saregama appointed Mr. Abhishek Kapoor as CFO effective July 15, 2026 — he is a CA with IIM-K executive specialisation, 25+ years of experience across M&E, FMCG, beverages/manufacturing, and was most recently CFO of Sula Vineyards (NSE-listed). Mr. Kuldeep Kothari (7-year Saregama insider) bridges as Interim CFO from June 24 to July 14. This is a meaningful upgrade — bringing in a CFO from a listed M&E/consumer company with strong capital markets experience signals Saregama may be planning strategic actions (fundraise, acquisition, or investor-facing initiatives). Prior notes flagged promoter SAST disclosures; a high-calibre external CFO hire alongside promoter share activity warrants close attention. Watch: Kapoor's first strategic move as CFO — any investor day, guidance revision, or M&A announcement in Q2 FY27.

notes.announcementTitle: Credit rating — CARE AA-/Stable reaffirmed; Rs130 Cr short-term facility rated A1+ (new)
notes.insight: CARE reaffirmed Saregama long-term bank facilities at CARE AA-/Stable (Rs100 Cr, reduced from Rs101.5 Cr — de minimis reduction) and separately assigned CARE A1+ to Rs130 Cr of short-term bank facilities (new assignment) while reaffirming CARE A1+ on existing Rs3.5 Cr short-term lines. The new Rs130 Cr A1+ short-term assignment is the material item — Saregama is either establishing or formalizing working capital lines, consistent with growth in content acquisition, digital licensing, or Carvaan manufacturing. AA-/Stable LT rating indicates very strong credit quality. No change in outlook or downgrade risk. Watch: whether the Rs130 Cr short-term line is drawn down materially in FY27 (visible in balance sheet borrowings) and what it funds.

NSE:AXISCADES.name: AXISCADES Technologies Ltd
NSE:AXISCADES.businessSummary: AXISCADES is an engineering R&D services company focused on aerospace, defense, automotive, energy, and industrial domains. It is undergoing a strategic transformation involving divestment of its heavy engineering/automotive/aerospace engineering services vertical.

notes.announcementTitle: Credit Rating placed on Rating Watch with Developing Implications
notes.insight: CARE Ratings has placed AXISCADES' long-term bank facilities (Rs. 21 Cr) and combined long/short-term facilities (Rs. 80 Cr) on Rating Watch with Developing Implications (RWD) — directly linked to the company's announced divestment of its Engineering Services business (heavy engineering, automotive, energy, aerospace) disclosed on May 26 and June 12, 2026. A RWD rating means the outcome — upgrade or downgrade — depends on how the divestment is executed and what the resulting company looks like. If the divested business is the core revenue engine, the remaining entity's credit profile could weaken significantly. Conversely, if proceeds are used to deleverage, it could be credit-positive. This is a transitional moment: watch for details on the divestment deal structure (valuation, buyer, retained business) and how management plans to redeploy the capital.

notes.announcementTitle: Q4 & FY26 earnings concall transcript — Power 930 roadmap, Phase 1 divestment signed, Xida Inc. created
notes.insight: AXISCADES Q4 & FY26 earnings transcript reveals a company in aggressive transformation. Key takeaways: (1) FY26 was a 'building year' — revenue deferred (not lost) due to supply chain disruptions and Phase 2 divestment delayed but still planned for H1 FY27; (2) Phase 1 divestment of engineering services (heavy engineering, energy, automotive) was signed the same week — proceeds to be reinvested into higher-margin aerospace/defense/electronics; (3) Devanahalli campus and Hyderabad Missile Atmanirbhar Complex (MAC) become operational in FY27, significantly expanding manufacturing/MRO footprint; (4) Aerospace manufacturing acquisition in advanced stages, expected to close in Q3 FY27; (5) Xida Inc. formed — Silicon Valley-headquartered deep tech/AI/hardware company targeting data center, GenAI, and physical AI markets with Indian/Vietnamese engineering; (6) Power 930 roadmap targets Rs9,000 Cr revenue by FY30 (massive stretch from current base). The CARE Rating Watch (Developing) from prior notes is consistent with the active divestment — ratings will be reviewed post-divestment close. Key risk: execution credibility given ambitious targets and deferred deliveries in FY26. Watch: Phase 2 divestment close timeline, Xida revenue ramp, and whether Q1 FY27 shows deferred revenue recognition.

notes.announcementTitle: Disclosures under Reg. 31(1) and 31(2) of SEBI (SAST) Regulations 2011 (Jupiter Capital)
notes.insight: Promoter Jupiter Capital Pvt Ltd (Rajeev Chandrasekhar group; holds 4,06,84,047 shares = 58.03% of capital) had a RELEASE of pledge over 86,000 equity shares (0.20% of capital) that were pledged with Tata Capital, effective 25-06-2026. Post-event, Jupiter's encumbered shares stand at ~25,51,000 (~6.0% of capital). Marginally POSITIVE but small — a 0.20% un-pledge is immaterial on its own; the residual ~6% pledge remains. Read PDF directly (scanned/OCR). Connection: continues the pattern of SAST pledge-related disclosures on this promoter (prior notes on file). What to watch: whether further pledge releases follow, indicating genuine promoter deleveraging, versus routine churn of collateral.

NSE:CONFIPET.name: Confidence Petroleum India Ltd
NSE:CONFIPET.businessSummary: Confidence Petroleum is one of India's largest LPG cylinder manufacturers and distributors, also engaged in automotive LPG/CNG retail and industrial gas solutions.

notes.announcementTitle: Investor Presentation
notes.insight: Confidence Petroleum filed an investor presentation with the exchanges on June 23, 2026. The PDF content was not extractable, limiting insight. Such presentations typically update investors on operational performance, capacity utilization, and near-term guidance. Watch for the uploaded version on the company website (www.confidencegroup.co) for specific KPIs — particularly LPG cylinder volumes, CNG station additions, and EBITDA margin trajectory.

NSE:RPTECH.name: Rashi Peripherals Ltd
NSE:RPTECH.businessSummary: Rashi Peripherals (RP Tech) is one of India's largest IT product distributors, representing global technology brands across compute, peripherals, networking, and components. It is pivoting from pure hardware distribution toward higher-margin enterprise and solutions.

notes.announcementTitle: Board Approves Acquisition of 67% stake in VDA Infosolutions Pvt Ltd for Rs. 368.5 Cr
notes.insight: RP Tech has approved acquisition of a 67% controlling stake in VDA Infosolutions Pvt Ltd (a Mumbai-based enterprise IT solutions and services company) for Rs. 368.5 crores cash consideration — implying an EV of ~Rs. 550 crores (~0.65x FY26 revenue of Rs. 850 Cr). VDA had net worth of Rs. 234 Cr and total assets of Rs. 427 Cr as of March 2026. This is a transformative move: RP Tech is buying a services/solutions company to accelerate its pivot from low-margin product distribution (~1-2% EBITDA) to higher-margin managed services and enterprise IT. The deal valuation looks reasonable at ~0.65x revenue for a profitable IT services firm. Key risks: integration complexity (distribution vs. services DNA), funded by cash (monitors balance sheet leverage) and execution risk in scaling VDA under RP Tech's umbrella. Watch for deal completion timeline, financing structure, and whether VDA's existing enterprise clients cross-sell RP Tech's distribution portfolio.

NSE:GODREJIND.name: Godrej Industries Ltd
NSE:GODREJIND.businessSummary: Godrej Industries is the holding company of the Godrej Group, with interests in chemicals (surfactants, oleo chemicals), estate, and financial investments in Godrej Consumer, Godrej Properties, and Godrej Agrovet.

notes.announcementTitle: Outcome of Postal Ballot — Resolution Passed with Requisite Majority
notes.insight: Godrej Industries' postal ballot dated May 15, 2026 has concluded with the resolution passed with requisite majority (voting closed June 22, 2026). The specific resolution detail was not captured in the filing summary — the full postal ballot notice from May 15 would reveal what shareholders approved (typically could be a related party transaction, fundraise, or governance change for a holding company like GIL). This confirms the board can proceed with whatever corporate action was proposed last month. Watch for the follow-through implementation announcement to understand the materiality.

notes.announcementTitle: Completion of Tenure of Independent Director Mr. Ajaykumar Vaghani
notes.insight: Godrej Industries has disclosed the completion of independent director Mr. Ajaykumar Vaghani's tenure. This is a routine governance disclosure — independent directors serve fixed terms under SEBI LODR. Watch for the announcement of a replacement independent director, as SEBI requires minimum independent director ratios. For a holding company with diverse group interests, the composition of the board's independent members can influence governance quality.

notes.announcementTitle: NCD allotment: ₹1,000 Cr (2 series × ₹500 Cr) at 8.23% p.a.; maturities Sep 2031 & Dec 2031
notes.insight: Godrej Industries allotted ₹1,000 Cr of listed, unsecured, redeemable NCDs on private placement — Series 1 (₹500 Cr, 63-month tenor, 8.23% p.a., matures Sep 24, 2031) and Series 2 (₹500 Cr, 66-month tenor, 8.23% p.a., matures Dec 24, 2031). Use of proceeds: general business purposes. For a Godrej Group holding company, this is a routine capital markets debt raise; 8.23% is at a modest spread to G-Sec given the Godrej name. The ₹1,000 Cr adds to the holding company's existing debt load — monitor consolidated debt levels in the FY26 annual report. No equity dilution, and coupon is annual. Low-signal for the operating businesses.

notes.announcementTitle: Further Investment of ~₹370 Cr in Godrej Investment Ltd (GIVL, WOS)
notes.insight: Godrej Industries has made a further equity investment of ~₹370 Crore in Godrej Investment Limited (GIVL), its wholly owned subsidiary incorporated January 5, 2026. GIVL is an Unregistered Core Investment Company that holds equity in Godrej Capital Limited (NBFC) and Godrej Wealth & Asset Management Limited — GIVL's consolidated income was ₹2,477.72 Crore from Jan 5 to March 31, 2026. This is the second round of capital infusion (previous disclosed January 28, 2026). The structure confirms Godrej Industries is systematically capitalizing its financial services holding company, building scale in lending (Godrej Capital) and wealth management. The transaction is arm's length and within shareholder-approved Section 186 limits. This is an intra-group capital allocation to a high-growth financial services platform — monitor Godrej Capital's AUM and loan book growth in upcoming filings.

NSE:HARDWYN.name: Hardwyn India Ltd
NSE:HARDWYN.businessSummary: Hardwyn India manufactures architectural hardware products including door fittings, glass fittings, and access control hardware. Listed on NSE SME platform.

notes.announcementTitle: Corrigendum to First EGM Notice — July 03, 2026
notes.insight: Hardwyn India has issued a corrigendum to its First Extraordinary General Meeting notice, scheduled for July 3, 2026 via VC/OAVM. Corrigenda to EGM notices typically correct an error in the original notice — often around resolution wording, eligibility dates, or voting details. The substance of what shareholders will vote on at the EGM remains unknown without reading the original notice. Watch for the EGM outcome on July 3 to understand what corporate action (fundraise, structural change, or special resolution) was being sought.

NSE:AEROENTER.name: Aeroflex Enterprises Ltd
NSE:AEROENTER.businessSummary: Aeroflex Enterprises manufactures stainless steel flexible hoses and assemblies for industrial, defense, and infrastructure applications.

notes.announcementTitle: NSE Sought Clarification on Significant Price Movement
notes.insight: NSE has formally sought clarification from Aeroflex Enterprises regarding significant movement in its stock price on June 23, 2026 — this is a surveillance action triggered when a stock moves sharply without a disclosed corporate reason. The company's reply is awaited. Such queries are typically followed by either a company confirmation that there is no undisclosed material information, or disclosure of a pending corporate event (order win, acquisition, fundraise, etc.). If the price spike was driven by speculation and no material event exists, expect a price correction. If a corporate event is disclosed in the reply, this could be a high-significance catalyst. Watch closely for the company's response filing within 24 hours.

NSE:APOLLO.name: Apollo Micro Systems Ltd
NSE:APOLLO.businessSummary: Apollo Micro Systems designs and manufactures defense and aerospace electronic systems — embedded electronics, avionics, and electronic warfare solutions primarily for Indian defense PSUs and DRDO.

notes.announcementTitle: Allotment of 21.43L Equity Shares on Conversion of Warrants at Rs. 114/share
notes.insight: Apollo Micro has allotted 21,43,095 equity shares at Rs. 114/share to three allottees (Reena Karwa, Superstar Investments, Trikaya Wealth Advisors) upon conversion of warrants originally issued June 2025. Total proceeds received: Rs. 18.32 crores. The conversion was at Rs. 85.50 warrant exercise price (25% paid upfront; remainder now). Paid-up capital increases to 37.16 crore shares — incremental dilution of ~0.58%, which is modest. This is a part-conversion of a larger batch of 3.8 crore warrants issued in 2025; remaining ~3.59 crore warrants are still outstanding and will result in further dilution over time. With the stock likely trading above Rs. 114, conversion is economically rational for warrant holders. Monitor the pace of remaining warrant conversions as a potential supply overhang on the stock.

NSE:SETL.name: Standard Engineering Technology Ltd
NSE:SETL.businessSummary: Sterling and Wilson Renewable Energy is a global solar EPC (Engineering, Procurement & Construction) company — one of the largest solar EPC players in India and internationally.

notes.announcementTitle: NSE Sought Clarification on Significant Price Movement
notes.insight: NSE has sought a clarification from Standard Engineering Technology Ltd on June 23, 2026 regarding significant movement in its stock price. This is a standard surveillance query for unusual price/volume activity. The company's reply is pending. For small-cap/SME companies like SETL, such queries can sometimes precede announcement of contract wins, promoter stake sales, or fundraising events. However, they can also reflect speculative activity with no underlying corporate development. Watch for the company's response and whether it is followed by a material disclosure.

notes.announcementTitle: Reply to Exchange Clarification on Price/Volume Movement
notes.insight: SETL has replied to an NSE/BSE clarification query on price/volume movement, stating there is no undisclosed material information causing the movement. Stock-specific clarification filings are routine when the exchange flags unusual price action — the important read is whether SETL hints at any upcoming announcement. Monitor stock price action and watch for any subsequent order win, fundraise, or JV disclosure that may have been the real catalyst.

notes.announcementTitle: Acquiring 51% in GScale Energy (AI Datacenter Engineering) for ₹190 Cr Phase 1; ₹500 Cr Total
notes.insight: SETL (formerly Standard Glass Lining Technology) has approved acquisition of up to 51% equity in GScale Energy Private Limited (incorporated May 15, 2026, CIN: U28199TS2026PTC216284) for ₹190 Crore Phase 1 (₹125 Cr cash + ₹65 Cr share swap), with a total committed ₹500 Crore program across equity, capex, and working capital — entirely self-funded from SETL's cash flows. GScale claims 486 MW of AI datacenter infrastructure delivered and 1 GW+ under execution, with hyperscaler relationships and LOIs in a $5.2-6.7 trillion global AI datacenter capex market by 2030 ($40-50 billion India). This explains the prior stock price movement and exchange clarification query — the deal was being structured. Key risks: GScale was incorporated only in May 2026 with zero historical revenue, making the 486 MW claim unverifiable from public sources; the ₹190 Cr acquisition price implies a very high valuation for a newly incorporated entity. The prior notes correctly flagged to watch for a major announcement following the price movement. SETL is pivoting from pharma/chemical glass lining to a dual-platform model (pharma engineering + AI datacenter). The strategic bet is bold but execution risk is high given GScale's lack of audited financials.

NSE:MARINE.name: Marine Electricals (India) Ltd
NSE:MARINE.businessSummary: Marine Electricals manufactures power distribution systems, switchgear, and electrical panels — primarily for marine vessels, data centers, and industrial applications.

notes.announcementTitle: Order win of Rs. 75.19 Cr from STT Global Data Centres and Princeton Digital Group
notes.insight: Marine Electricals has won two orders totalling Rs. 75.19 crores (ex-tax) from STT Global Data Centres India and Princeton Digital Group India — both marquee data center operators — for supply of power distribution systems over 12-18 months. This is a meaningful order in the context of India's booming data center capex cycle, and confirms Marine Electricals is establishing itself as a credible power distribution vendor for hyperscale/co-location DCs. Two separate blue-chip data center clients in a single announcement suggests a broadening customer base beyond traditional marine applications. Watch for whether data center revenue becomes a recurring order stream and how this compares to total order book / annual revenue to gauge materiality.

NSE:GPIL.name: Godawari Power & Ispat Ltd
NSE:GPIL.businessSummary: Godawari Power & Ispat is an integrated steel manufacturer based in Chhattisgarh, producing iron pellets, sponge iron, billets, and wire rods. It also has captive power generation.

notes.announcementTitle: 6.91 MW Waste Heat Recovery Power Plant Commences Commercial Operations
notes.insight: GPIL's 6.91 MW Waste Heat Recovery Based (WHRB) power plant at its Siltara, Raipur facility has commenced commercial operations on June 23, 2026 — announced earlier on April 2, 2026. The plant utilises waste heat from the Pellet Plant and Ferro Alloys Division flue gas, converting it into captive power. This reduces GPIL's grid power dependency and lowers per-unit energy costs, which is directly margin-accretive for a steel manufacturer where power is a major input cost. While 6.91 MW is relatively modest in the context of an integrated steel plant, it represents a step toward operational self-sufficiency. Watch for total captive power capacity post this addition and management commentary on power cost savings per tonne in the next quarterly result.

NSE:HONASA.name: Honasa Consumer Ltd
NSE:HONASA.businessSummary: Honasa Consumer (Mamaearth parent) is a digital-first House of Brands in beauty and personal care — operating Mamaearth, Derma Co, Aqualogica, Dr. Sheth's, Staze, and BBlunt. It sells primarily via D2C and modern trade with a digital-first go-to-market.

notes.announcementTitle: Acquisition of 58% stake in Fluence Pharma Pvt Ltd at ~Rs. 135 Cr EV; WOS Honasa Health incorporated
notes.insight: Honasa has acquired a controlling 58% stake in Fluence Pharma Pvt Ltd — a nutraceuticals company with patented Cyclical Nutrition Therapy (CNT), dermatologist distribution in 3,000+ clinics, and brands Hair Fact/Skin Fact/Pro Fact — at ~Rs. 135 crores EV (implying ~3.4x FY26 revenue of Rs. 40 Cr and ~17x EV/EBITDA at 20% margins). The remaining 42% will be acquired in two tranches over 5-7 years. Honasa will also incorporate 'Honasa Health Private Limited' as a WOS to run B2C nutraceuticals operations. This marks a deliberate strategic pivot: Honasa is moving beyond topical beauty into 'inside-out' supplements, tapping India's Rs. 16,000 Cr+ vitamins and supplements market growing at 11% CAGR. The acquisition is competitively logical — Derma Co already has a clinical-dermatologist channel, and Fluence's practitioner trust (3,000+ dermatologists) directly complements it. Key risks: nutraceuticals is a new category with different regulatory and formulation complexity; valuation multiples appear full for a Rs. 40 Cr revenue company; management bandwidth stretched across 6+ brands. Watch for contribution of nutraceuticals revenue in Q2/Q3 FY27 and whether the Fluence science can scale via D2C channels.

notes.announcementTitle: Arbitration rectification order — total award upward revised to AED 9.92 million (~Rs25.5 Cr) against RSMM
notes.insight: Following the May 14, 2026 arbitral award against ex-distributor RSMM General Trading LLC (Dubai), Honasa filed a Section 33 rectification application to fix computational errors. The Tribunal allowed it on June 26, 2026 — revising the loss of profits upward from AED 4.34M to AED 7.32M (59% increase, due to wrong FY cited) and correcting substitution costs from AED 1.06M to AED 0.74M. Final total award is AED 9.92 million (~Rs25.5 Cr), plus RSM must indemnify Honasa for any Dubai court recovery and is restrained from continuing Dubai proceedings. Interest at EIBOR+2% applies if unpaid after 30 days. Rs25.5 Cr is modest relative to Honasa revenues but the Dubai indemnification clause provides open-ended protection. The win resolves an international distributor dispute cleanly. Watch: whether RSM pays the award or contests it in Dubai, and any further Dubai court proceedings.

NSE:KIRLOSENG.name: Kirloskar Oil Engines Ltd
NSE:KIRLOSENG.businessSummary: Kirloskar Oil Engines (KOEL) manufactures diesel and gas engines, gensets, and pump systems for agriculture, industrial, and power generation applications. It is a leading engine maker with export presence.

notes.announcementTitle: Multiple Investor Call Intimations — June 26 and June 29, 2026
notes.insight: KOEL has scheduled 5 separate investor calls over June 26 (at 2:45 PM, 3:30 PM, 4:15 PM) and June 29 (at 10:30 AM, 11:15 AM) — an unusually high density of investor meets in a short period. This pattern is consistent with a management roadshow, often timed around quarterly results or a significant corporate development that management wants to proactively communicate to institutional investors. Watch for whether a Q1 FY27 result, analyst day, or corporate action announcement follows these calls, which could explain the urgency.

notes.announcementTitle: Investor meet outcomes — 1x1s with Aditya Birla MF, WhiteOak AMC, Axis MF on June 26
notes.insight: KOEL completed three more 1x1 investor calls on June 26, 2026 (Aditya Birla MF at 2:45 PM, WhiteOak AMC at 3:30 PM, Axis MF at 4:15 PM), all on operational overview with no UPSI disclosed. Combined with prior intimation, KOEL has conducted at least 5 investor meetings across June 26 and June 29. The density of 1x1s with large domestic MFs (Aditya Birla, Axis) and boutique (WhiteOak) in a compressed window is consistent with a post-results NDR or pre-event investor roadshow. No new information is public from these meetings. Watch: any management commentary, concall transcript, or investor presentation that may emerge to explain the sudden surge in investor engagement.

NSE:SIS.name: SIS Ltd
NSE:SIS.businessSummary: SIS Ltd is one of India's largest security services and facility management companies, operating across guarding, cash logistics (SIS Cash Services), and electronics security. It has significant operations in Australia via SIS Australia.

notes.announcementTitle: Board to Consider Buyback of Equity Shares on June 29, 2026
notes.insight: SIS Ltd's board has scheduled a meeting on June 29, 2026 to consider and approve a buyback of equity shares. A buyback signals management's confidence in the company's intrinsic value exceeding the current market price and is shareholder-friendly — it reduces share count, boosts EPS, and returns cash more tax-efficiently than dividends. For SIS, this comes at a time when the security services sector is experiencing steady demand growth from corporates and government contracts. The buyback size, method (open market vs. tender offer), and pricing will be critical — watch for the board outcome on June 29 to assess materiality. If the buyback price is at a meaningful premium to CMP, expect a sharp positive price reaction.

notes.announcementTitle: Disclosures under Reg. 31(1) and 31(2) of SEBI (SAST) Regulations 2011 (Harshbeena Sahney Zaveri)
notes.insight: Promoter & Vice-Chairman/MD Harshbeena Sahney Zaveri (holds 4,02,07,885 shares = 41.48% of capital) RELEASED pledges in two tranches: (1) 23-06-2026 unpledge of 37,84,427 shares (3.90% of capital) after fully prepaying her Aditya Birla Capital loan — pledged shares to ABCL are now ZERO; (2) 25-06-2026 unpledge of 7,84,000 shares (0.81%) on prepayment of excess shares to Tata Capital. Net effect: her encumbered shares fell from 1,10,68,681 (11.42% of capital) to 65,00,254 (~6.70%), i.e. encumbrance as a % of her own holding dropped from ~27.5% to ~16.2%. Clearly POSITIVE — promoter deleveraging and unwinding of pledge reduces forced-sale/overhang risk and signals improved personal liquidity. Read PDF directly (OCR). What to watch: whether the residual ~6.7% pledge is also cleared in coming quarters, and the source of funds used for prepayment.

BSE:KMEW.name: Knowledge Marine & Engineering Works Ltd
BSE:KMEW.businessSummary: Knowledge Marine & Engineering Works (KMEW) is a marine and dredging company providing services for inland waterways, ports, and coastal infrastructure. It charters dredgers and marine vessels.

notes.announcementTitle: Board Meeting June 26 to Approve Equity Fundraise (Preferential/QIP/Warrants)
notes.insight: KMEW has called a board meeting on June 26, 2026 to consider raising funds via equity shares, warrants, preference shares, or a combination through preferential allotment, QIP, or private placement — subject to shareholder approval via EGM. The board will also call an EGM. This is a clear equity fundraise signal — KMEW is likely looking to fund fleet expansion or working capital given the ongoing boom in Indian coastal and inland waterway infrastructure spending. The fundraise route (QIP vs. preferential) will indicate whether institutional investors are being targeted. Watch for the board outcome on June 26 for quantum, method, and pricing.

notes.announcementTitle: SAST Reg 29(2): Promoters sold 5,12,820 shares (2.10%) via Bulk Deal on open market
notes.insight: Promoters Kanak Kewalramani (sold 3,45,620 shares; 29.22%→27.81%) and Dinesh Kewalramani (sold 1,67,200 shares; 3.35%→2.66%) sold a combined 5,12,820 shares (2.10% of total capital of 2,44,44,108 shares) via a bulk deal on the open market on June 19, 2026. Total promoter group holding fell from 53.66% to 51.56% — a meaningful −2.10% reduction in a single transaction. CRITICAL CONTEXT: The prior note (June 2026) flagged a board meeting on June 26 to consider a fresh equity fundraise (QIP/preferential/warrants). This promoter selling immediately before a fundraise announcement is a significant negative signal — promoters appear to be reducing exposure ahead of dilutive fundraising. The combination of promoter selling + looming equity issuance creates a double dilution risk for minority shareholders. Watch: outcome of June 26 board meeting (fundraise quantum and instrument), whether additional promoter selling occurs, and the CMP reaction post board meeting.

notes.announcementTitle: Board approved Rs150 Cr preferential issue at Rs1,962.53/share + allotted 1.56 lakh shares on warrant conversion
notes.insight: KMEW board approved two equity actions on June 26, 2026: (1) Preferential issue of up to 7,64,317 shares at Rs1,962.53/share to non-promoter investors, raising Rs150 Cr (EOGM on July 19, 2026 for shareholder approval; CARE Ratings appointed as monitoring agency). (2) Allotment of 1,55,892 equity shares to promoter Sujay Kewalramani on warrant conversion at Rs1,900/share exercise price (75% balance paid = Rs142.5 Cr/share x 1.56 lakh = Rs11.1 Cr total). Post-conversion, paid-up equity is 2.46 Cr shares. The Rs150 Cr preferential raise at Rs1,962.53/share to non-promoters is the key event — it signals institutional or strategic interest at a specific price anchor and will dilute existing shareholders by ~3.1% (7.64 lakh / ~2.46 Cr post). Watch: identity of allottees in Annexure I (not shown in this filing), use of Rs150 Cr proceeds, and whether EOGM passes on July 19.

NSE:LLOYDSENGG.name: Lloyds Engineering Works Ltd
NSE:LLOYDSENGG.businessSummary: Lloyds Engineering Works manufactures industrial and process equipment for oil & gas, power, and defence.

notes.announcementTitle: Promoter Lloyds Enterprises Releases Pledge on 2.3 Crore Shares (LAS with Tata Capital)
notes.insight: Promoter entity Lloyds Enterprises Limited has released a pledge on 2,30,00,000 equity shares of Lloyds Engineering Works (held as security under a Loan Against Securities agreement with Tata Capital), effective June 16, 2026. Pledge release is a strong positive signal — it means the promoter has repaid or restructured the LAS borrowing, reducing the overhang of forced selling that pledged shares represent. Lower promoter pledge = lower risk of margin calls triggering market sales. Watch for total remaining promoter pledge levels post this release to assess whether the overhang is fully cleared.

notes.announcementTitle: EGM Scheduled July 15, 2026
notes.insight: Lloyds Engineering Works has called an Extraordinary General Meeting (EGM) on July 15, 2026. EGMs are called for matters requiring urgent shareholder approval — typically equity fundraise (preferential allotment/warrants), significant acquisitions, change of objects, or key management appointments. Watch for the agenda/notice to understand the strategic intent.

NSE:NAVINFLUOR.name: Navin Fluorine International Ltd
NSE:NAVINFLUOR.businessSummary: Navin Fluorine is a specialty fluorochemicals manufacturer producing refrigerants, specialty fluorides, CDMO/CRAMS services, and high-performance products for agrochem, pharma, and semiconductor sectors.

notes.announcementTitle: Board Meeting Intimation for Quarterly/Annual Results
notes.insight: Navin Fluorine has intimated a board meeting to consider and approve quarterly/annual financial results. Given Navin Fluorine's ongoing CDMO capacity ramp at Surat and recovery in specialty fluorochemical demand, the results call will be closely watched for: CDMO revenue recognition, HPP segment margin trajectory, and management commentary on order pipeline from global agrochem/pharma customers.

NSE:SHILPAMED.name: Shilpa Medicare Ltd
NSE:SHILPAMED.businessSummary: Shilpa Medicare is an Indian pharma company focused on oncology APIs, formulations, biologics, and CDMO services, with operations across 80+ countries. Its material subsidiary Shilpa Biologicals is a biologics/ADC CDMO platform.

notes.announcementTitle: Shilpa Biologicals commissions ADC GMP Manufacturing Facility
notes.insight: Shilpa Biologicals (material subsidiary of Shilpa Medicare) has commissioned an end-to-end Antibody-Drug Conjugate (ADC) GMP Drug Substance manufacturing facility at Dharwad, making Shilpa one of very few Indian companies with fully integrated ADC capabilities — covering payload synthesis, linker development, mAb production, conjugation, and purification. GMP qualification protocols are now underway, placing the facility on a commercial readiness path (typically 6–18 months from commissioning to first commercial batch). ADC manufacturing is a high-margin, rapidly growing segment globally (~$20B market by 2030, driven by cancer biologics); Shilpa's 25-year HPAPI heritage is a genuine differentiator for cytotoxic payload handling. No revenue numbers or customer names disclosed in the press release — watch for: first commercial CDMO contract announcement, GMP approval from USFDA/EMA, and revenue contribution from Shilpa Biologicals segment in FY27 results.

notes.announcementTitle: Shilpa Biologicals — Settlement with Sartorius Stedim India for ₹9 Cr
notes.insight: Shilpa Biologicals (material subsidiary) settled a commercial dispute with Sartorius Stedim India Pvt. Ltd. before Bengaluru Commercial Court for ₹9 Cr on June 23, 2026 — against Sartorius's original claim of ₹20.13 Cr (~55% haircut for the claimant). The ₹9 Cr outflow is a one-time charge to Shilpa Biologicals; not material to consolidated Shilpa Medicare P&L but worth flagging given Sartorius is a key bioprocessing equipment/consumables supplier used in biologics manufacturing — notably this dispute arose mid-build of the ADC facility (OS No. 153/2024, filed 2024). Settlement removes a supplier relationship overhang just as the ADC facility commissions (announced same day), which is strategically important — it signals the Sartorius relationship is normalized for future GMP qualification consumables. Watch: whether Sartorius remains a vendor for the ADC facility going forward, and any further litigation disclosures from Shilpa Biologicals.

NSE:ARVIND.name: Arvind Ltd
NSE:ARVIND.businessSummary: Arvind Ltd is one of India's largest textile companies — manufacturing denim, woven fabrics, and garments. It also has real estate and retail ventures.

notes.announcementTitle: Alteration in Memorandum of Association
notes.insight: Arvind Ltd has altered its Memorandum of Association. MoA alterations typically reflect an expansion of business objects — potentially into new segments, geographies, or business lines. For a textile company, MoA amendments could indicate diversification into technical textiles, defense textiles, or adjacent manufacturing. Watch for the specific object clause amendment to understand the strategic intent.

NSE:INDSWFTLAB.name: Ind-Swift Laboratories Ltd
NSE:INDSWFTLAB.businessSummary: Ind-Swift Laboratories manufactures Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) and finished dosage forms for global regulated and semi-regulated markets.

notes.announcementTitle: Resignation of Director
notes.insight: Ind-Swift Laboratories has disclosed a director resignation. Director departures at pharma companies should be monitored carefully — for independent directors, a resignation before term completion can sometimes signal governance concerns. Watch for whether this is an independent director, executive director, or promoter-group director, and the stated reason for resignation.

notes.announcementTitle: Change in Management
notes.insight: Ind-Swift Laboratories has disclosed a change in senior management. Combined with the director resignation filed separately, this represents meaningful leadership transition. For an API/pharma company, management stability is critical for US FDA compliance, customer relationships, and R&D continuity. Watch for the nature of the change — a new CEO/CFO appointment vs. a departure — and whether it aligns with the company's ongoing expansion plans.

NSE:SYRMA.name: Syrma SGS Technology Ltd
NSE:SYRMA.businessSummary: Syrma SGS is an EMS (Electronics Manufacturing Services) company making PCBs, electronic assemblies, and IoT devices for healthcare, automotive, and industrial customers. It has been expanding into higher-complexity electronics.

notes.announcementTitle: JV Agreement with Kaga Electronics India for EMS Facility Targeting Japanese Clients
notes.insight: Syrma SGS has signed a JV agreement with Kaga Electronics India Pvt Ltd to establish a state-of-the-art EMS manufacturing facility in India, focusing specifically on Japanese OEM clients. Syrma will hold up to 60% and Kaga up to 40% in the JV entity (to be incorporated). Kaga Electronics is a major Japanese electronics distributor/manufacturer — this JV is a deliberate strategy to capture Japan-to-India EMS relocation as Japanese companies diversify supply chains away from China. This is strategically significant: Japanese customers tend to be long-term, volume-driven, and exacting on quality — winning a marquee Japanese JV partner validates Syrma's manufacturing capabilities. Watch for JVCo incorporation, initial capex, and first Japanese client order announcement.

notes.announcementTitle: CEO change — Jaidit Singh Brar (ex-McKinsey Senior Partner) appointed CEO effective June 29, 2026
notes.insight: Syrma SGS appointed Jaidit Singh Brar as CEO effective June 29, 2026, replacing Satendra Singh who moves to a new role of Senior Operating Director (lateral move, not an exit). Brar is an IIM Calcutta MBA and ex-Senior Partner at McKinsey with 25+ years of experience in P&L management, turnaround, and transformation; he currently serves as independent director on Exide Industries and Tenneco Clean Air boards. This is a significant leadership upgrade — bringing in a McKinsey senior partner as CEO signals a shift toward professionalizing growth strategy, especially relevant given the recent Kaga Electronics JV for Japanese OEM EMS manufacturing. Retaining Satendra Singh as Senior Operating Director provides operational continuity. Watch: Brars first 100-day priorities (order book growth vs margin improvement vs JV execution) and whether McKinsey-style restructuring triggers any cost or portfolio rationalization.

NSE:SATIN.name: Satin Creditcare Network Ltd
NSE:SATIN.businessSummary: Satin Creditcare is a mid-size NBFC-MFI focused on microfinance lending to low-income borrowers, primarily rural women in Tier 3-5 markets. It operates in a high-NPA risk environment tied to borrower stress cycles.

notes.announcementTitle: Board Approves NCD Issuance of Up to Rs. 5,000 Crore; AGM Aug 7, 2026
notes.insight: Satin Creditcare's board approved raising up to Rs. 5,000 Crore via privately placed NCDs in one or more tranches over 1 year, subject to shareholder approval at the AGM (Aug 7, 2026). This is a significant liability-side event — Rs. 5,000 Cr is a very large borrowing authorization for an NBFC of Satin's size, suggesting aggressive AUM growth plans for FY27. For an MFI, NCD-based borrowing diversifies away from bank credit lines which can be restrictive. The key risk: microfinance NPA cycle in India has been elevated — watch whether the growth plan is backed by improving collection efficiency or risks amplifying leverage into a stressed borrower base. Also noted: CCO Vikas Gupta re-appointed for 3 years, and AGM set for Aug 7.

NSE:MUTHOOTMF.name: Muthoot Microfin Ltd
NSE:MUTHOOTMF.businessSummary: Muthoot Microfin is a microfinance NBFC-MFI primarily operating in South and East India, offering small-ticket JLG loans to rural women borrowers. It is part of the Muthoot Pappachan Group.

notes.announcementTitle: Board Meeting June 30 to Authorize NCD Issuance Rs. 3,000 Cr (Private) + Rs. 1,000 Cr (Public) for FY27
notes.insight: Muthoot Microfin is calling a board meeting on June 30, 2026 to approve NCD issuance of up to Rs. 3,000 Crore via private placement and Rs. 1,000 Crore via public issue for FY2026-27 — total Rs. 4,000 Crore in authorized borrowings. AGM scheduling also on the agenda. This suggests Muthoot Microfin is planning significant AUM expansion in FY27 and wants capital markets access beyond bank credit. The public NCD route (Rs. 1,000 Cr) is notable — public NCDs attract retail investors and require higher disclosure standards. Watch for board outcome on June 30 and AGM date announcement.

BSE:YASHHV.name: Yash Highvoltage Ltd
BSE:YASHHV.businessSummary: Yash Highvoltage manufactures high-voltage electrical equipment including transformers, switchgear, and power transmission components. It operates in the fast-growing power infrastructure segment.

notes.announcementTitle: Board Approves Preferential Allotment of Shares + Warrants at Rs. 721/share totalling Rs. 151 Cr; EGM July 15
notes.insight: Yash Highvoltage board approved a preferential issue of 12.62L equity shares and 8.32L convertible warrants to non-promoter identified allottees at Rs. 721/share (face value Rs. 5, premium Rs. 716), raising Rs. 151 Crores in total. EGM called for July 15, 2026 for shareholder approval. The relevant date for ICDR pricing purposes is June 15, 2026. This is a significant equity fundraise for a power equipment company — Rs. 151 Cr at Rs. 721/share to non-promoters indicates institutional interest. Watch for who the allottees are (disclosed in Annexure A of the board outcome), whether this capital is earmarked for capacity expansion, and whether the warrants get converted at a later date (potential further dilution of ~8.32L shares).

NSE:DEEDEV.name: DEE Development Engineers Ltd
NSE:DEEDEV.businessSummary: DEE Development Engineers manufactures specialized process piping, pressure vessels, and industrial equipment for oil & gas, power, and other sectors. Its WOS DEE Fabricom India has expanded into wind energy structures.

notes.announcementTitle: WOS DEE Fabricom Wins Rs. 64 Cr Order for 15 Windmill Towers from Ganeko Solar
notes.insight: DEE Development's wholly owned subsidiary DEE Fabricom India has bagged an order worth Rs. 64 Crores (inclusive of GST) from Ganeko Solar Pvt Ltd for manufacturing 15 Envision-make EN156 353MT windmill towers for 3.3MW turbines, to be executed by January 2027. This is a notable order for the company's renewable energy vertical — wind tower manufacturing is a high-value structural engineering play benefiting from India's accelerating wind energy capacity additions. Payment terms are structured (25% advance + 55% on material readiness + 20% within 15 days), which is healthy for working capital. Watch for whether this signals a pipeline of similar wind tower orders and how DEE Fabricom's revenue contribution grows relative to the parent DEE Development.

notes.announcementTitle: Proceedings of the EGM held on June 27, 2026
notes.insight: At an EGM held 27-Jun-2026 (1:00-1:20 pm via VC), shareholders voted on a single Special Resolution: 'Preferential Issue of Securities on Private Placement basis'. The proceedings confirm the item was transacted; the actual voting results and Scrutinizer's Report will follow separately, and the size/price/allottees of the preferential issue are in the EGM Notice dated 03-Jun-2026 (not reproduced in this proceedings document — read PDF directly). Why it matters: a preferential issue is a fresh equity fundraise that DILUTES existing shareholders but brings growth/working-capital capital — relevant given DEE's order-led expansion (e.g. the prior Rs.64 cr DEE Fabricom windmill-tower order). Direction depends on price vs CMP and identity of allottees (promoter vs strategic/marquee investor = more positive signal). What to watch NEXT: (1) the voting-results filing to confirm passage; (2) the issue price, number of shares/warrants, dilution %, and allottee names; (3) end-use of proceeds.

notes.announcementTitle: Preferential Issue of Securities approved by shareholders at EGM (27-Jun-2026)
notes.insight: What happened: At its first EGM (27-Jun-2026, via VC), DEEDEV shareholders PASSED the single special resolution - 'Preferential Issue of Securities on a Private Placement basis' - with the requisite majority (e-voting; record date 20-Jun, 44,061 shareholders on record). The Scrutinizer (Kapil Kumar & Co) certified passage; exact for/against percentages are in the attached e-voting table. Why it matters: this clears the final shareholder gate for a fresh equity fundraise - dilutive to existing holders but brings growth/working-capital funds, directionally relevant given DEE's order-led expansion. The decisive terms (instrument, number of securities, issue/conversion price, allottee names/type, dilution %, use of funds) are NOT in this PDF - they sit in the EGM Notice dated 03-Jun-2026 (+ 18-Jun corrigendum) and were not captured in our notes; read those for the price-vs-CMP and promoter-vs-strategic-investor signal. Context: this completes the approval step flagged on 27-Jun (prior note: EGM proceedings) and follows the Rs 64 cr DEE Fabricom windmill-tower order (23-Jun) - consistent with a capital-raising-for-growth narrative. What to watch next: the allotment / PAS-3 filing confirming final issue price, share/warrant count, resulting dilution %, and allottee identity (promoter skin-in-the-game vs pure third-party dilution).

notes.announcementTitle: Outcome of EGM dated 27-Jun-2026
notes.insight: Formal outcome-of-EGM disclosure (Reg 30/44): same content as the preferential-issue approval filed the same day - the single special resolution 'Preferential Issue of Securities on a Private Placement basis' was passed with requisite majority at the 27-Jun-2026 EGM (record date 20-Jun; 92 shareholders attended via VC). This is the procedural confirmation/e-voting-results disclosure for the fundraise covered in the companion note today; no new business terms beyond passage. Direction: same as the fundraise - dilutive but growth-funding; terms (price/allottees/quantum) remain in the 03-Jun EGM Notice. What to watch next: the allotment filing for final issue price, dilution %, and allottee identity.

NSE:MSPL.name: MSP Steel & Power Ltd
NSE:MSPL.businessSummary: MSP Steel & Power is a mid-size integrated steel manufacturer in Eastern India producing billets, TMT bars, and structural steel. It has captive power and is focused on the construction steel market.

notes.announcementTitle: 5 Promoter Group Entities Buy ~46.7L Shares in Open Market on June 16-18, 2026
notes.insight: Five promoter group entities of MSP Steel (Shree Vinay Finvest, Ginny Traders, Sampat Marketing, Ilex Pvt Ltd, M.A. Hire Purchase) collectively purchased ~46.7 lakh equity shares in the open market between June 16-18, 2026 — a coordinated, multi-entity open market buy by the promoter group. This is a strong signal of promoter conviction in the stock at current prices. Such coordinated promoter buying often precedes a re-rating catalyst (better quarterly results, order wins, capacity commissioning) or reflects a view that the stock is undervalued. Also note: MSPL has called an EGM for July 14, 2026 for unspecified special business — the combination of promoter buying + EGM could indicate an impending corporate action. Watch for the EGM agenda disclosure and Q1 FY27 results.

notes.announcementTitle: EGM Convened for July 14, 2026
notes.insight: MSPL has called an EGM for July 14, 2026 (from June 19 disclosure) for special business, though the specific agenda items from the EGM notice were not fully captured. Combined with the promoter group buying 46.7L shares in open market on June 16-18, this EGM takes on additional significance — could involve fundraise approval, structural change, or a strategic resolution. Watch for EGM notice details and outcome on July 14.

notes.announcementTitle: 3 Promoter Entities Buy ~21L Shares Open Market (June 19-22, 2026)
notes.insight: Three promoter group entities of MSP Steel purchased ~20.97 lakh equity shares in the open market on June 19-22, 2026: Ginny Traders Pvt Ltd (7.30L on Jun 19), Sampat Marketing Company Pvt Ltd (12.04L across Jun 19 & 22), and Ilex Private Limited (1.67L on Jun 22). This is the second consecutive round of promoter purchases — the prior note recorded ~46.7L shares bought by 5 promoter entities on June 16-18, 2026. The combined 2-week promoter accumulation now stands at ~67.7L shares, a very strong and sustained insider buying signal. Combined with the EGM scheduled for July 14, 2026, this accumulation pattern suggests promoters are positioning ahead of a potentially value-accretive event. Watch the EGM agenda and any further SAST disclosures as the trigger date approaches.

NSE:TDPOWERSYS.name: TD Power Systems Ltd
NSE:TDPOWERSYS.businessSummary: TD Power Systems manufactures AC generators, alternators, and motors for power generation — for gas turbines, steam turbines, hydro applications, and industrial use. It exports significantly and serves global OEMs.

notes.announcementTitle: CRISIL Upgrades Credit Rating to AA-/Stable (from A+/Positive); Short-Term to A1+
notes.insight: CRISIL has upgraded TD Power Systems' long-term bank facility rating from A+/Positive to AA-/Stable, and short-term from A1 to A1+, on Rs. 488 Crore of bank facilities. A credit upgrade to AA- is a meaningful signal — it indicates CRISIL has assessed a material improvement in TDPS's financial risk profile, likely reflecting stronger cash flows, improved order book quality, reduced leverage, or better coverage ratios. AA- is investment-grade excellent, enabling TDPS to borrow at lower rates and enhancing supplier/customer confidence. This upgrade also reflects the strong tailwinds in global power generation demand (both conventional and renewable). Watch for the detailed CRISIL rationale letter for specific improvement drivers — whether it is order book, export revenues, or balance sheet deleveraging.

NSE:BANDHANBNK.name: Bandhan Bank Ltd
NSE:BANDHANBNK.businessSummary: Bandhan Bank is a private sector bank with strong roots in microfinance lending (MFI), primarily serving low-income borrowers in Eastern India. It has been diversifying into retail, MSME, and housing finance.

notes.announcementTitle: Board Meeting June 25 to Discuss Capital Plan
notes.insight: Bandhan Bank has called a board meeting on June 25, 2026 to discuss the bank's capital plan. For a bank, a formal capital plan board meeting can signal one of several things: (1) a capital raise via QIP, preferential allotment, or AT1 bonds to shore up capital adequacy ratios; (2) a strategic pivot in capital allocation toward specific segments; or (3) RBI-driven capital planning requirements. Bandhan has historically had capital adequacy concerns tied to its MFI book stress. Watch for board outcomes on June 25 — the quantum, instrument type (equity vs. debt), and pricing will be critical. An equity raise would signal dilution; AT1/Tier-2 would signal leverage path.

notes.announcementTitle: Appointment of Non-Executive Chairman and Review of Capital Plan
notes.insight: Bandhan Bank has appointed Mr. Debasish Panda (1987 UP-cadre IAS) as Non-Executive Chairman effective July 5, 2026, for 3 years (subject to shareholder approval). Panda is a heavyweight: ex-Chairman IRDAI (2022-2025) where he drove 'Insurance for All by 2047' and ex-Secretary DFS (2020-2022) who led bank consolidations. His appointment — pre-approved by RBI — is a strong governance signal for the bank which has been under scrutiny for elevated stress. On the capital plan, the Board concluded the bank is well-capitalized and does not need additional capital now, though it has in-principle advised initiating discussions for future growth capital. The prior note (from June 22 board intimation) anticipated this outcome. The combination of a high-calibre regulatory-insider Chairman and a no-dilution capital stance is positive near-term for the stock. Watch for Panda's first public statements and whether Q1FY27 results show asset quality improvement.

NSE:ACUTAAS.name: Acuitas Solutions Ltd
NSE:ACUTAAS.businessSummary: Acuitas Solutions (formerly known as Acutaas) is an IT services company providing enterprise software, ERP, and digital transformation services.

notes.announcementTitle: CGST Anti-Evasion Search Conducted June 23, 2026 — Inquiry Concluded, No Demand Issued
notes.insight: The CGST (Central Goods & Services Tax) Anti-Evasion wing conducted a search at Acuitas' premises on June 23, 2026. The company has disclosed that the inquiry has concluded and no demand has been issued as of the date of filing. While the absence of a tax demand is reassuring, the fact of a CGST anti-evasion search is itself a red flag worth monitoring — it indicates GST authorities had specific intelligence that triggered the search. Watch for any follow-up disclosures indicating a show-cause notice or demand in subsequent weeks, as GST inquiries can continue post-search.

NSE:SEAMECLTD.name: Seamec Ltd
NSE:SEAMECLTD.businessSummary: Seamec is a marine services company operating multi-support vessels (MSVs) for offshore oil & gas sector in India. It provides subsea services, diving support, and offshore construction.

notes.announcementTitle: Business Update Announcement under Reg 30
notes.insight: Seamec has filed a Regulation 30 business update — likely a contract win, vessel deployment update, or operational milestone. For an offshore services company operating in a tight vessel charter market, any contract extension or new charter is incrementally positive for revenue visibility. Monitor for details on charter rates and contract duration.

BSE:DECNGOLD.name: Deccan Gold Mines Ltd
BSE:DECNGOLD.businessSummary: Deccan Gold Mines is India's first and only listed gold and critical minerals company, developing the Jonnagiri Gold Project in Andhra Pradesh (via subsidiary GMSI) and holding exploration assets across AP, Karnataka, Kyrgyzstan, Chhattisgarh, Spain, Mozambique, Finland, and Tanzania.

notes.announcementTitle: Availing Loan of Rs. 3 Crore from Modali Consultants LLP
notes.insight: Deccan Gold has taken a Rs. 3 Crore loan from Modali Consultants LLP. For a pre-revenue exploration company, related-party or promoter-connected loans are the primary funding mechanism. At this stage, the amount is small relative to exploration capex needs. Monitor for: interest rate terms, repayment schedule, whether Modali is a promoter-connected entity, and whether the borrowing triggers RPT approval requirements.

notes.announcementTitle: Jonnagiri Gold Project Inaugurated by AP Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu
notes.insight: The Jonnagiri Gold Project (Andhra Pradesh) was formally inaugurated by AP Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu on June 24, 2026 — a significant political and operational milestone for Deccan Gold's subsidiary GMSI. Current capacity: ~1,000 tonnes of ore/day producing 400–600 kg of gold annually; expansion to ~2,500 tonnes/day (1+ tonne/year gold) has been announced, with the CM laying the foundation stone for the next plant phase and extending state support for expansion. This is a major de-risking event: government-level endorsement significantly reduces regulatory/political risk for the project, and the village renaming to 'Swarnagiri' signals deep political backing. Prior note showed Deccan Gold was borrowing ₹3 Cr from related party (Modali Consultants LLP), indicating thin cash; watch whether state government support translates into land/water clearances for expansion capex and whether the operating GMSI subsidiary eventually merges or lists. Watch: production ramp timeline, revenue/cash flow from GMSI to parent Deccan Gold, and capex funding mechanism for the 2,500-tpd expansion.

NSE:SOMANYCERA.name: Somany Ceramics Ltd
NSE:SOMANYCERA.businessSummary: Somany Ceramics manufactures and sells ceramic and vitrified tiles for residential and commercial use. It is one of India's top 3 tile manufacturers.

notes.announcementTitle: Credit Rating Announcement
notes.insight: Somany Ceramics has filed a credit rating update under Reg 30. Watch for whether the rating is affirmed, upgraded, or downgraded — and the agency's commentary on ceramic sector demand recovery. The tiles sector has seen margin pressure from rising input costs (gas, clay) and competitive pricing. Any upgrade would indicate improved cash flow generation; a downgrade would raise red flags on leverage.

NSE:KRISHANA.name: Krishana Phoschem Ltd
NSE:KRISHANA.businessSummary: Krishana Phoschem manufactures single super phosphate (SSP) fertilizers and is a mid-size agrochemical company.

notes.announcementTitle: SEBI Takeover Regulations Disclosure — Promoter/PAC Stake Change
notes.insight: Krishana Phoschem has filed a SEBI Takeover (SAST) Regulations disclosure, indicating a change in promoter or PAC shareholding. Multiple SAST disclosures in one day could indicate systematic buying or acquisition activity. Watch for cumulative stake changes — if promoters are buying, it may signal undervaluation conviction; if selling, watch for dilution risk.

notes.announcementTitle: Stock split 1:5 (₹10 FV → ₹2 FV); Record Date July 3, 2026
notes.insight: Krishana Phoschem will sub-divide each equity share of ₹10 face value into 5 shares of ₹2 face value, with Record Date July 3, 2026 (this Friday). Post-split, shares outstanding will increase 5x and the trading price will theoretically reduce to ~1/5th of current price — improving liquidity and retail accessibility. The AGM proceedings (same date) would have ratified this split resolution. Action required: if holding, be aware that from ex-date (July 2 likely) the stock trades at the adjusted price and post-record date you receive 5 shares per share held. No change to intrinsic value.

NSE:KIRLOSBROS.name: Kirloskar Brothers Ltd

Reinstatement fully restores Jindal Saw's export competitiveness in the seamless pipe segment. This is a clear positive — the overhang from the license suspension is now removed. A reaffirmation at AA/Stable signals no credit deterioration post FY26 results, but no improvement either. AA (not AA+) on a pipes company with significant exposure to oil & gas and infra suggests moderate leverage. The Rs500 Cr NCD stock is material for a mid-cap and the back-loaded repayment schedule (2029-31) reduces near-term cash flow pressure. Watch: any rating action closer to FY28 when repayment planning would begin, and order book trends vs debt trajectory.
