Watch for quantification of Site 5 capacity (tonnes per annum) and whether Q1FY27 concall provides revenue ramp-up guidance from the new facility.

notes.announcementTitle: Outcome Of The Meeting Of The Fund Raising Committee — Allotment (QIP)
notes.insight: Tanfac Industries completed its QIP on June 25, 2026, allotting 12,58,918 equity shares at ₹1,985.83/share (5% discount to floor price), raising ~₹250 Crore. Post-allotment paid-up capital rises from ₹9.975 Cr (1.995 Cr shares) to ₹10.6 Cr (2.121 Cr shares) — ~6.3% equity dilution. Major allottees: Malabar India Fund (18% of issue), Motilal Oswal India Excellence Fund combined series (25.99%), Turnaround Opportunities Fund (8%), Niveshaay Hedgehogs Fund (7%), Bengal Finance (10%). The participation of quality institutional investors — Malabar (known for long-term fundamental investing) and multiple Motilal Oswal schemes — signals strong institutional conviction in Tanfac's growth prospects. Watch for how the raised capital is deployed (capex, working capital, debt reduction) in upcoming quarterly disclosures and management commentary.

notes.announcementTitle: AGM Outcome: ED Re-appointment and Chairman Continuation
notes.insight: Apcotex Industries' 40th AGM (June 25, 2026) approved two governance items: (1) Re-appointment of Ravishankar Sharma (35+ years experience in specialty chemicals manufacturing) as Executive Director for 2 years, effective May 1, 2026 to April 30, 2028. (2) Continuation of Atul Choksey (ex-MD of Asian Paints 1984-1997) as Non-Executive Chairman post age-75 under SEBI Reg 17(1A). Both outcomes confirm management stability — no leadership disruption. No strategic announcements were made at the AGM. Continue monitoring for the Q1FY27 results and any capex or product pipeline update.

notes.announcementTitle: SVP-R&D Rishi Kumar Mishra Resigned
notes.insight: Mr. Rishi Kumar Mishra, Senior Vice President – Research & Development (designated SMP), resigned from ACME Solar effective June 25, 2026 to pursue external career opportunities. The R&D function leadership exit at a solar holding company at a time when technology differentiation (perovskite, bifacial, storage integration) is becoming a competitive moat is worth monitoring. No replacement has been announced. This is a medium-level concern — SVP is below CXO level and ACME Solar's core business is project development/EPC rather than manufacturing R&D. Watch for whether a replacement is named and whether this signals any strategic de-prioritization of in-house R&D.

NSE:SPARC.name: Sun Pharma Advanced Research Company Ltd

notes.announcementTitle: License Agreement with CMS Bridging DMCC (China/HK) Terminated
notes.insight: SPARC has mutually terminated its November 5, 2019 license agreement with CMS Bridging DMCC, which covered development and commercialization of five products — Xelpros™ (latanoprost ophthalmic), Elepsia™ (levetiracetam), Taclantis™ (tacrolimus), PDP-716, and SDN-037 — in Mainland China, Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan. The company states there is no material financial impact on operations. However, this is a negative strategic signal: SPARC is exiting the China market licensing pipeline for 5 molecules (some already commercialized), which curtails the China revenue and royalty upside thesis that was part of SPARC's value. The mutual termination may reflect either CMS's inability to commercialize (regulatory failure), or SPARC choosing to seek a better partner or go direct. Watch for any re-licensing announcement for the China market and SPARC management's commentary on the China pipeline in the next concall.

notes.announcementTitle: Senior management change — COO Dr. Nitin Dharmadhikari and HR Head retire June 30; new HR Head appointed
notes.insight: SPARC's Chief Operating Officer Dr. Nitin Dharmadhikari and HR & Administration Head Ms. Shanta Gupta both retire effective June 30, 2026. Prasanna Deshmukh (TISS HRM graduate) is appointed as the new HR Head from July 1, 2026 — but no COO replacement is announced. Losing a COO at a clinical-stage specialty pharma company is meaningful: SPARC's value is driven by its pipeline assets and regulatory timelines, and operational continuity at the C-suite is important. The absence of a named COO successor raises a question about whether the role is being restructured or a search is underway. Watch: announcement of COO successor or restructuring of responsibilities, and any pipeline update that could be linked to operational transition.

notes.announcementTitle: Deputy COO Cabins & Fabrication Division Resigned After 11.5 Years
notes.insight: Shri Rashmi Mohan Shrivastva, Deputy COO of the Cabins & Fabrication Division (an SMP), resigned from Sandhar Technologies on June 25, 2026 citing personal reasons, after 11.5 years with the company. His last working day is July 24, 2026. The Cabins & Fabrication division serves the farm equipment and construction vehicle segment. A 11.5-year tenure suggests institutional knowledge loss, though this is below the CXO level. No replacement has been named. This follows a broader pattern of mid-management exits in auto ancillary companies amid slowing farm equipment demand. Monitor the division's performance in upcoming results and whether a replacement is announced.

notes.announcementTitle: Postal Ballot: Suchita Jain as VC & MD, Neeraj Jain as MD Approved
notes.insight: Vardhman Textiles members approved via postal ballot (ended June 25, 2026): (1) Mrs. Suchita Jain (DIN 00746471) as Vice-Chairperson & Managing Director for 5 years w.e.f. April 15, 2026, and (2) Mr. Neeraj Jain (DIN 00340459) as Managing Director for 5 years w.e.f. April 15, 2026. This is a leadership succession formalization within the founding family, with a dual MD structure. Both appointments signal continuity of promoter control and a generational handover at Vardhman, one of India's largest yarn manufacturers. The 5-year terms provide management stability. Watch for any strategic shifts in capital allocation (capex for capacity, new geographies) under the new leadership structure.

notes.announcementTitle: Postal ballot — Mrs. Suchita Jain appointed Vice-Chairperson & MD; Mr. Neeraj Jain appointed MD (both passed)
notes.insight: Vardhman Textiles postal ballot (voting closed June 25, 2026) passed two ordinary resolutions: (1) Mrs. Suchita Jain appointed as Vice-Chairperson & Managing Director — 100% votes in favour from promoter group (97.5% of promoter shares voted); (2) Mr. Neeraj Jain appointed as Managing Director — both passed with requisite majority. This is a succession/governance event at the Vardhman group — the Jain family (promoters) formalizing the next generation into MD-level roles. Both are promoter-family members (Suchita is an interested party per the disclosure). This is standard family-business succession in Indian textiles and does not represent external professional management. Watch: any strategic shifts in capital allocation or expansion decisions under the new MD structure.

notes.announcementTitle: Promoters Vikas Jain & Piyush Jain Sold 11.66L Shares (3.91% stake) in Open Market
notes.insight: Promoters Vikas Jain and Piyush Jain sold 11,65,820 equity shares (3.91% of total capital / 3.57% diluted) via open market on June 22-23, 2026. Pre-sale combined promoter holding: 68.85% (2,05,38,783 shares). Post-sale: 64.94% (1,93,72,963 shares). Mode: open market. The promoters also hold 22,69,288 convertible warrants (from Feb 2025 preferential allotment). This is a bearish signal — a 3.91% stake disposal in 2 days is sizeable for a small-cap. Prior last disclosure was February 23, 2025, meaning no promoter selling was recorded for ~16 months before this sudden exit. The trigger for selling is unknown — could be personal liquidity, rebalancing, or concerns about sector outlook. Monitor whether selling continues in subsequent weeks via further SAST filings, and watch whether the warrants are exercised or lapsed.

notes.announcementTitle: Promoter CMD Srinivasan Ravi Sold 5.25L Shares (2.01%) in Open Market
notes.insight: Mr. Srinivasan Ravi, Promoter, Chairman & Managing Director of Craftsman Automation, sold 5,25,000 equity shares (2.01% of total capital of 2,61,54,433 shares) via open market on June 23, 2026. Pre-sale holding: 1,05,00,000 shares (40.15%). Post-sale: 99,75,000 shares (38.14%). This is a high-significance bearish signal — it is the CMD himself selling, not a PAC or non-executive promoter. A 2% stake disposal by the founding CMD is material and suggests either personal liquidity needs or a view that the stock is fully valued. Craftsman Automation operates in auto components (powertrain, aluminium casting, storage solutions) and has been a quality mid-cap compounder. No prior SAST selling was disclosed in the notes. Watch for any further selling disclosures and whether the CMD participates in upcoming analyst/investor meets to address this.

notes.announcementTitle: Board Refresh: 4 IDs Re-appointed, WTD Vijay Ananth Re-appointed for 5 Years
notes.insight: Data Patterns' board approved multiple reappointments at the June 25, 2026 meeting: (1) Sowmyan Ramakrishnan (ex-Tata group) — ID, 3 years from Sep 10, 2026; (2) Prasad Raghava Menon (ex-SKF/Axis Bank) — ID, 3 years from Sep 10, 2026; (3) Anuradha Sharma (HR specialist) — ID, 1 year from Sep 10, 2026; (4) Mathew Cyriac — re-designated as Independent Director, 5 years from Jul 31, 2026; (5) Vijay Ananth K — re-appointed as Whole-Time Director, 5 years from Jul 31, 2026. Outgoing: Sastry Vadlamani retires Sep 9, 2026 and will not seek re-appointment. This is a stable board refresh with high continuity. WTD Vijay Ananth's 5-year re-appointment is the most material item — it confirms management stability for the medium term in a defence electronics company with lumpy order execution. Routine outcome; no strategic signal.

notes.announcementTitle: Lumel SA (WOS, Poland) Wins Forbes Diamonds 2026 for 2nd Consecutive Year
notes.insight: Rishabh Instruments' wholly owned Polish subsidiary Lumel SA has received the Forbes Diamonds 2026 award (Poland's leading business ranking for financial strength and growth) for the second consecutive year — the 5th prestigious award in 18 months. Lumel operates in the PLN 50-250M revenue category (medium enterprise) in industrial automation, measurement, and energy management. The award validates Lumel's consistent execution and operational excellence. No new financial data was disclosed. This is a positive brand-building development that reinforces the thesis of Rishabh's European platform building via Lumel. Monitor Lumel's revenue/EBITDA contribution in RISHABH's annual report for FY26 to assess the financial significance of this recognition.

NSE:APOLLOPIPE.name: Apollo Pipes Ltd
NSE:APOLLOPIPE.businessSummary: Apollo Pipes Ltd (APL) manufactures and trades PVC, HDPE and LDPE pipes, fittings, bathroom fittings, water storage, and door/window solutions. FY26 turnover ~Rs845 Cr, net worth ~Rs887 Cr. Competes with Astral and Prince Pipes in plastic pipes.

notes.announcementTitle: Scheme of Arrangement - Amalgamation with Kisan Mouldings Ltd (KML)
notes.insight: Apollo Pipes board approved a two-stage amalgamation on June 26, 2026: first KML Tradelinks Pvt Ltd (KTPL, shell with Rs0.01 Cr turnover) merges into its parent Kisan Mouldings Ltd (KML, Rs119 Cr turnover, Rs250 Cr net worth); then KML merges into Apollo Pipes (APL, Rs845 Cr turnover, Rs887 Cr net worth) with appointed date April 1, 2026. KML shareholders receive APL equity at a swap ratio set by registered valuers (Axiology Valuetech) and validated by merchant banker Corporate Professionals Capital — the actual ratio is not disclosed yet. Since KML is already APL subsidiary, this simplifies group structure by absorbing a same-sector PVC pipes business (Kisan and KML Classic brands in agri, construction, household) representing ~14% of APL revenue. Requires SEBI, NSE, BSE, NCLT, and shareholder approval (typically 9-15 months). Watch: actual share exchange ratio disclosure (key valuation/dilution event) and whether KMLs Rs250 Cr net worth carries hidden liabilities.

NSE:BLUSPRING.name: Bluspring Enterprises Ltd
NSE:BLUSPRING.businessSummary: Bluspring Enterprises Ltd operates through STEAG Energy Services (India) Pvt Ltd (step-down subsidiary) providing comprehensive O&M services for thermal power plants. Listed in 2024, it is an energy services company focused on long-duration O&M contracts.

notes.announcementTitle: Order win - O&M contract extension, 600 MW TPP, Vedanta Power Ltd, Rs406 Cr
notes.insight: STEAG Energy Services (India), Blusprings step-down subsidiary, secured two O&M contract extensions from the Vedanta group on the same day: (1) Rs406.43 Cr for 600 MW TPP O&M from Vedanta Power Ltd; and (2) Rs1,219.85 Cr for 1,800 MW TPP O&M (Units 1, 3 & 4) from Vedanta Aluminium Metal Ltd. Both are 5-year contracts effective July 1, 2026. Combined new order inflow is Rs1,626.28 Cr over 5 years, implying ~Rs81 Cr/quarter in recurring O&M revenue. These are contract extensions (originally awarded by Vedanta Ltd), confirming customer retention and visibility of long-duration revenue. Vedanta group represents a high-concentration client risk to watch. Watch: total order book size post-extension and whether new logos beyond Vedanta are being added.

NSE:INDOTECH.name: Indo Tech Transformers Ltd
NSE:INDOTECH.businessSummary: Indo Tech Transformers Ltd manufactures power and distribution transformers up to 400 KV. Current capacity 14,000 MVA (80-90% utilized), serving India power sector capex boom.

notes.announcementTitle: Capacity addition — Rs360 Cr CAPEX approved, targeting 50,000 MVA by March 2029
notes.insight: Indo Tech Transformers board approved a fresh Rs360 Cr CAPEX on June 26, 2026 to expand capacity from the current 14,000 MVA to 50,000 MVA (400 KV range) by March 2029. This is the fourth CAPEX tranche — cumulative spend approved is now Rs495 Cr (Rs75 Cr Dec-24, Rs25 Cr Nov-25, Rs35 Cr May-26, Rs360 Cr now). Existing utilization is 80-90%, providing strong justification for expansion. The 3.6x capacity increase (14,000 to 50,000 MVA) is aggressive and reflects confidence in India power sector demand; financing via internal accruals and term loans signals moderate leverage risk. This is a step-change in ambition — the previous tranches were incremental (to 16,000 → 20,000 → 25,000 MVA), but this Rs360 Cr single approval doubles the prior cumulative commitment. Watch: debt levels as capex is executed over FY27-29, order book trajectory to validate the capacity build, and whether transformer price cycles support IRR on Rs495 Cr spend.

notes.announcementTitle: Credit rating — CARE AAA/Stable reaffirmed on NCDs and bank facilities; Rs450 Cr NCD withdrawn on full redemption
notes.insight: CARE reaffirmed Great Eastern Shipping at CARE AAA/Stable across all instruments — the highest possible rating. Rs450 Cr NCD tranche was withdrawn as it was fully redeemed (positive — clean debt paydown). Remaining rated debt: Rs300 Cr NCDs (reduced from Rs300 Cr, reaffirmed AAA), Rs600 Cr NCDs (reaffirmed AAA), and Rs50 Cr LT/ST bank facilities at AAA/A1+. Full redemption of the Rs450 Cr NCD tranche signals strong free cash flow generation; the company is actively deleveraging its NCD book. AAA rating at a shipping company is exceptional and reflects the conservative balance sheet strategy. Watch: fleet expansion plans or any new borrowing that could alter the clean balance sheet trajectory.

NSE:GREENPLY.name: Greenply Industries Ltd
NSE:GREENPLY.businessSummary: Greenply Industries is a leading plywood and wood panel manufacturer. Expanding into MDF via WOS Greenply Speciality Panels Pvt Ltd (GSPPL).

notes.announcementTitle: Board approved Rs130 Cr corporate guarantee to Federal Bank for subsidiary GSPPL MDF plant capex
notes.insight: Greenply board approved a Rs130 Cr corporate guarantee in favour of Federal Bank for a Capex Letter of Credit to be availed by wholly-owned subsidiary Greenply Speciality Panels Pvt Ltd (GSPPL) for machinery procurement for MDF plant capacity expansion. The LC is valid 12 months from issuance; Greenply will charge guarantee commission from GSPPL. This confirms active MDF capex execution at the subsidiary level — GSPPL financials are consolidated into Greenply, so the Rs130 Cr capex will flow through the group balance sheet. A contingent liability of Rs130 Cr at the parent level is manageable but watch for GSPPL cash flow ramp-up post-commissioning to service the credit facility. MDF expansion is strategically important as Greenply transitions from a pure plywood play toward engineered wood panels. Watch: GSPPL MDF plant commissioning timeline, whether the Rs130 Cr LC is drawn in full, and GSPPL revenue/EBITDA contribution at consolidation.

notes.announcementTitle: CTO appointment — Vijay L Thange (ex-USV, Cipla, RPG Life Sciences, 36 yrs pharma ops experience)
notes.insight: Kopran appointed Vijay L Thange as CTO effective June 26, 2026 — 36 years of pharma manufacturing experience including VP Plant Operations at USV, and stints at Cipla and RPG Life Sciences. Creating a CTO role at a mid-cap pharma API/formulations company signals focus on manufacturing quality, capacity scaling, or USFDA/EUGMP compliance preparation. Thange's plant operations background (vs. R&D) suggests the priority is operational excellence rather than new molecule development. Watch: any USFDA inspection announcements, capacity expansion disclosures, or quality compliance actions at Kopran plants that may be linked to this hire.

NSE:PAISALO.name: Paisalo Digital Ltd
NSE:PAISALO.businessSummary: Paisalo Digital is a listed NBFC (BSE: 532900 / NSE: PAISALO) focused on small-ticket lending to the under-banked, operating a co-lending/business-correspondent model with banks alongside its own balance-sheet book across MSME, personal and rural/agri loans. Promoter group is led by the Agarwal family (Sunil & Santanu Agarwal) via entities including Equilibrated Venture Cflow Pvt Ltd.

notes.announcementTitle: Disclosure of reasons for encumbrance by promoter under Reg. 31(1) r/w Reg. 28(3) SEBI (SAST) 2011 (Equilibrated Venture Cflow)
notes.insight: Promoter-group entity Equilibrated Venture Cflow Pvt Ltd (holds 19,39,89,880 shares = 21.33% of capital) disclosed reasons for its encumbrance: a small RELEASE of pledge over 74,000 shares (0.01% of capital) by pledgee Cholamandalam Investment & Finance on 25-06-2026, leaving 6,41,32,000 shares (7.05% of capital) still pledged. Stated reason: the pledge was created for availment of a loan used for conversion of equity warrants. Across the whole promoter group, encumbered shares equal 33.06% of promoter shareholding (NOT 50%+, and below 20% of total capital). Read PDF directly. Assessment: mildly NEGATIVE/neutral context — sizeable one-third of promoter holding remains pledged and the borrowing funded warrant conversion (promoter leverage to increase stake). The 74,000-share release itself is immaterial. What to watch: trajectory of the 7.05% residual Chola pledge and whether warrant-conversion-linked leverage rises further.

notes.announcementTitle: Disclosures under Reg. 31(1) and 31(2) of SEBI (SAST) Regulations 2011 (Equilibrated Venture Cflow)
notes.insight: Formal Reg. 31(1)/31(2) SAST disclosure covering the SAME underlying event as the same-day encumbrance-reasons filing: promoter-group entity Equilibrated Venture Cflow Pvt Ltd (19,39,89,880 shares = 21.33%) had a Cholamandalam pledge RELEASE of 74,000 shares (0.01%) on 25-06-2026, leaving 6,41,32,000 shares (7.05% of capital) pledged. Other promoters' pledges unchanged (Sunil Agarwal 95,81,000 / 1.05%; Santanu Agarwal 96,02,000 / 1.06%; Pro Fitcch 71,96,000 / 0.79%; Pri Caf 72,26,000 / 0.79%). Read PDF directly. Duplicate-event filing, so LOW incremental significance beyond the companion note. Assessment: neutral — confirms continued ~7% promoter pledge with Chola tied to warrant-conversion borrowing. What to watch: same as companion note — reduction of the residual pledge.

NSE:ADFFOODS.name: ADF Foods Ltd
NSE:ADFFOODS.businessSummary: ADF Foods is a manufacturer and exporter of ethnic Indian packaged foods - pickles, chutneys, ready-to-eat meals, frozen foods, sauces and spices - sold under brands such as Ashoka, Truly Indian, Aeroplane and PJ's. The bulk of revenue comes from exports to North America, UK/Europe and the Middle East, with a growing domestic and food-service presence. ADF Foods (USA) Ltd is a step-down wholly owned subsidiary.

notes.announcementTitle: Intimation Under Regulation 30 - Favourable litigation order (Ascot Valley Foods vs ADF Foods USA)
notes.insight: What happened: The US District Court (SDNY) issued Findings of Fact/Conclusions of Law in the Ascot Valley Foods vs ADF Foods (USA) Ltd dispute - a substantially favourable order. ADF (USA) is held entitled to disgorgement damages of US$2,298,114 (~Rs 19 cr) representing Ascot's profits from misappropriating ADF's proprietary recipes/trade secrets through 31-Dec-2025, with further amounts to be computed for 2026 onward; ADF's own liability (for the co-pack breach claim) is capped at just US$100,706.96 plus interest. Why it matters: Net positive - removes a litigation overhang, validates protection of ADF's proprietary recipes/IP, and points to a potential ~US$2.2m+ recovery, though management explicitly flags NO immediate P&L impact as the order is subject to final judgment and enforcement. Magnitude is modest relative to company size, so it is a sentiment/IP-protection win more than a near-term earnings driver. Context: First note on this company. What to watch: ADF must file a proposed judgment + permanent injunction; parties have 14 days to confer/raise objections - watch for the final judgment, any Ascot appeal, and whether/when amounts are actually recovered and recognised.

NSE:PERSISTENT.name: Persistent Systems Ltd
NSE:PERSISTENT.businessSummary: Persistent Systems is a Pune-based global digital engineering and IT services company providing software product engineering, enterprise modernization and AI-led digital transformation to enterprises and software product companies, with major verticals in BFSI, healthcare/life sciences and technology. The bulk of revenue comes from North America.

notes.announcementTitle: Short-notice investor call (28-Jun-2026) on Persistent-Nagarro Business Combination & takeover offer for Nagarro SE
notes.insight: What happened: Persistent called a SHORT-NOTICE investor/analyst webcast for Sunday 28-Jun-2026 at 11:30 AM IST - deliberately scheduled before Indian and German markets reopen - to discuss a just-signed Business Combination Agreement with Nagarro to form the 'Persistent - Nagarro Group', explicitly including a VOLUNTARY PUBLIC TAKEOVER OFFER for Germany-listed Nagarro SE and the strategic rationale. Why it matters: this is a potentially transformative cross-border M&A, not a routine investor meet - it combines two large digital-engineering firms into a global AI-led player, materially changing Persistent's scale and geographic mix (adds Nagarro's European/German footprint). Direction/magnitude hinge on deal economics and financing, which could involve significant cash outlay, debt, or equity dilution. Context: first note on Persistent - flagged high to track closely. NOTE: the deal terms (offer price per Nagarro share, total deal size, cash/stock structure, financing, minimum acceptance threshold, expected close, regulatory approvals) are NOT in this PDF - they sit in the 27-Jun press release (ref 067) and intimation (ref 066); read those. What to watch next: the 28-Jun call for deal economics; offer price vs Nagarro SE's market price; how Persistent funds the offer (cash/debt/equity = dilution risk); and the German takeover-law timeline, regulatory clearances and minimum-acceptance conditions.
